subject: Biology
title: C3, C4, and CAM Plants
description: A quick, practical guide to C3, C4, and CAM Plants.

hook.text: Not all plants photosynthesize the same way. Some evolved clever tricks to handle heat and drought.

buildup.text: C3 plants use the standard Calvin cycle. They thrive in cool, moist climates.

discovery.text: C4 plants like corn pre-concentrate CO₂, reducing water loss in hot sunny environments.

twist.text: CAM plants like cacti open stomata only at night to save water, storing CO₂ for daytime use.

climax.text: These adaptations let plants colonize deserts, tropics, and grasslands that C3 plants can't handle.

punchline.text: Same goal, three strategies. Plants adapt or die.

quiz.question: When do CAM plants open their stomata?
